title: Prompts That Actually Work
goal: Write clear prompts that get usable results on the first try.

blocks.html:
A working prompt isn't a magic phrase. It's four plain parts, and when you include them you stop getting vague junk back.

  • Role — who the AI should act as (for example, 'you are a coffee shop owner').
  • Context — the situation ('launching a new oat-milk latte').
  • Task — the exact thing to do ('write 3 Instagram captions').
  • Format — what the answer should look like ('each under 15 words, friendly').

Leave parts out and the AI guesses. Guessing is where slop comes from. Try the quick check below — pick an answer and you'll see exactly why it works or doesn't.

blocks.question: Which of these prompts will actually get you a usable result?

options.explain: Too vague. No role, no real task, no format — you'll get generic filler.

options.text: You are a coffee shop owner launching an oat-milk latte. Write 3 Instagram captions, each under 15 words, friendly tone.
options.explain: Role, context, a clear task, and a format with limits. That's a prompt that returns something you can actually post.
There's nothing to act on — no task, no context. The AI has to invent everything.
A topic, but no role, no count, no length, no tone. The model still has to guess most of it.

lessons.title: Good vs Bad Prompt, Side by Side
